record.field: Your get-ready guide for the 1 July cutover. Work through each step against the live system — every item is "can you actually do this," not "did you read it." When you and a director have signed off, you're cleared to operate.
A1 · Log in & set your password

field.name: html_content
field.p: Go to crm.neonhiring.com, log in with the address IT gave you, set a strong password on first login, and confirm you reach your home screen. If you can't get in, flag Robin or Tatenda before cutover.
✅ Done when: logged in, password set, apps visible.

field.name: html_content
field.p: When you log in you land on your Sales Dashboard — your daily command centre. It shows your sales picture at a glance: pipeline value and deals by stage, leads, hot deals worth pushing, quotes that are aging (sitting too long without movement), what you've won, and your win rate — plus a tasks list and alerts. Start each morning here: it tells you what needs chasing today.

p.em: Your exact tiles adapt to your role.

field.p: ✅ Done when: you can open your Sales Dashboard and know it's your daily starting point.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Every enquiry becomes a lead — if it's not in the CRM, it doesn't exist. Leads reach you two ways: WhatsApp enquiries auto-create a lead, and you manually create leads for enquiries by phone, email, or in person. Capture the client, contact, and what they need.
✅ Done when: you can create a lead manually, and know WhatsApp enquiries arrive as leads automatically.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Your deals live on the Pipeline as cards. Keep each deal in the stage that reflects reality — the stages are New → Proposal Sent → Negotiation → Closed Won / Lost, plus Payment Pending Verification. Accurate stages keep the pipeline honest and trigger the right follow-ups. Move cards by drag-and-drop on the kanban, or via the status bar inside the deal.
✅ Done when: you can move a deal between stages both ways and know what each stage means.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Open a deal and log what happened — a call summary, a meeting note, a scheduled follow-up activity. Same-day, every time. This is how the system and your manager see a deal is being worked.
✅ Done when: you've logged a note and scheduled a follow-up activity on a deal.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Raise a quote through the Neon quote flow. It prices from the live catalogue / pricing rules — not guesswork. Build the quote with line items and watch it price; it carries a currency (USD or ZWG, locked per quote) and moves through its states (Draft → Pending Approval → Approved → Sent to Client → Accepted).

Get the quote right before it goes out — when a client accepts, it sets BOTH operations and finance in motion at once (the event starts being planned and finance picks up invoicing). A wrong quote becomes everyone's problem the moment it's accepted.
✅ Done when: you've produced a quote priced from the catalogue and understand acceptance triggers ops + finance together.

record.field: B4a · Confirm logistics before sending a quote

field.name: html_content
field.p: Before any quote goes to a client, you must confirm the logistics cost and include it. Logistics is a quotable line (the LOGISTICS product). You don't estimate it yourself — get the figure from Operations (currently Robin) and add it as the logistics line. A quote does not go out without logistics confirmed. This matters because acceptance triggers operations and finance at once (B4) — a quote missing logistics becomes a margin problem instantly.
This is a firm rule now and will become a built-in system check; until then, it's on you to confirm logistics every time before sending.
✅ Done when: you know to get the logistics figure from Ops and add the LOGISTICS line before any quote is sent, and that no quote goes out without it.

field.name: html_content
field.p: Your quotes live under My Quotation — the Neon quote list, where you find, reopen, and track your own (QUO-USD / QUO-ZIG numbered, with state and total).
✅ Done when: you can list and open your own quotes.

field.name: html_content
field.p: A custom discount above 10% needs MD approval — you can't apply it yourself. Know the rule before you promise a client a price.
✅ Done when: you can state the discount threshold (above 10% needs MD approval) and how approval is requested.

field.name: html_content

ul.li: Respond to clients within 2 hours during business hours.
Formal English in all written client communication.
Never quote verbally — all pricing goes through an official Neon quote.
Send a proposal within 24 hours of a client meeting.
Follow up every proposal within 3 business days if no response.
Log every client interaction same-day in the CRM.

field.p: ✅ Done when: you can recite these without looking.

field.name: html_content

ul.li: Copy Munashe on all client email correspondence — not just proposals, regardless of value.
A custom discount above 10% goes to the MD for approval.
The promise every client should feel from Neon: Confidence, Clarity, Pride.

field.p: ✅ Done when: you know to copy Munashe on all client emails, what to escalate, and what we promise clients.

record.field: An enquiry comes in by phone. First action?
Quote a rough price to move fast
Create a lead in the CRM — if it's not in the CRM, it doesn't exist
Correct — every enquiry becomes a lead first.
A WhatsApp enquiry arrives. What happens?
Nothing until you manually enter it
It auto-creates a lead — WhatsApp enquiries are captured automatically
Correct — WhatsApp enquiries arrive as leads automatically.
It goes straight to a quote
How is pricing given to a client?
Only through an official Neon quote, priced from the catalogue
Correct — pricing only ever goes out as an official Neon quote.
A client asks for a discount above 10%. Can you grant it yourself?
Yes, if it wins the deal
No — a discount above 10% needs MD approval
Correct — a discount above 10% needs MD approval; it's the MD's call.
Maximum response time to a client during business hours?
Correct — respond within 2 hours in business hours.
A proposal you sent 3 business days ago has had no reply. What do you do?
Follow up — every proposal is followed up within 3 business days
Correct — proactive follow-up within 3 business days.
Which client emails must copy Munashe?
Correct — copy Munashe on all client email, regardless of value.
Before you send a quote, what must you confirm?
The logistics cost — get the figure from Ops and add the LOGISTICS line; a quote doesn't go out without it
Correct — logistics confirmed and added before any quote is sent.
A client accepts a quote. What happens next?
It triggers both sides at once — operations begins planning the event, and finance picks up invoicing from the accepted quote
Correct — acceptance sets ops and finance in motion together.
The deal is closed, nothing more to do

field.name: html_content
field.p: Completed by a director (Robin or Munashe), confirming the rep can independently capture a lead, work the pipeline, log interactions, raise a catalogue-priced quote with logistics confirmed, find their quotes, and that they know the response/follow-up/escalation/discount rules and what quote acceptance sets in motion.
When a director marks this slide complete, Sales cutover onboarding is signed off and recorded. The rep is cleared to operate.
